summary: External-Dependency-Info.txt still lists libmicrohttpd (lines 1475-1988) even though PR #3463 removed the dependency from Skia's DEPS file; this stale entry is shipped as THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt in every NuGet package.

bugSignals.errorMessage: libmicrohttpd license text present in External-Dependency-Info.txt despite dependency removal

stepsToReproduce: Note the dependency is still listed despite being removed in PR #3463

fixStatus.reason: The libmicrohttpd section (lines 1475-1988) is still present in External-Dependency-Info.txt on the main branch. PR #3463 removed it from DEPS but did not update this file.

analysis.summary: PR #3463 (merged 2026-01-27) correctly commented out microhttpd in externals/skia/DEPS but did not remove the corresponding license section from External-Dependency-Info.txt. This file is packed as THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt in all NuGet packages via binding/NativeAssets.Build.targets line 17, so every shipped package still claims libmicrohttpd as a dependency.
analysis.rationale: The reporter correctly identified a stale license entry. This is a legitimate metadata bug: the dependency was removed but the license file was not updated. The fix is trivial — remove lines 1475-1989 from External-Dependency-Info.txt. Additionally, scripts/guardian/CredScanSuppressions.json has 6 stale microhttpd suppressions that should also be cleaned up. Other removed dependencies (imgui, SPIR-V Headers, SPIR-V Tools) may also need review.

keySignals.text: PR 3463 removed the libmicrohttpd dependency from Skia
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly references the PR that removed the dependency.

keySignals.text: it still appears in the External-Dependency-Info.txt
keySignals.interpretation: Confirmed: lines 1475-1988 still contain the full libmicrohttpd license text.

codeInvestigation.finding: Contains the full libmicrohttpd license text (LGPL/eCos dual license) spanning 513 lines, despite the dependency being removed from DEPS.
The microhttpd entry is commented out: '# third_party/externals/microhttpd'. Confirms the dependency was removed in PR #3463.
External-Dependency-Info.txt is packed as THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt in every NuGet package, meaning the stale entry ships to all consumers.
Contains 6 stale credential scan suppressions referencing externals/skia/third_party/externals/microhttpd paths that no longer exist.

resolution.hypothesis: PR #3463 removed microhttpd from DEPS but missed updating External-Dependency-Info.txt and CredScanSuppressions.json.

proposals.title: Remove libmicrohttpd section from External-Dependency-Info.txt
proposals.description: Delete lines 1474-1989 (the libmicrohttpd section including surrounding separator lines) from External-Dependency-Info.txt.

proposals.title: Clean up stale CredScanSuppressions
proposals.description: Remove the 6 microhttpd-related entries from scripts/guardian/CredScanSuppressions.json (lines 4-27) since the referenced files no longer exist.

proposals.title: Audit other removed dependencies
proposals.description: Several other dependencies removed in PR #3463 (imgui, SPIR-V Headers, SPIR-V Tools) also still appear in External-Dependency-Info.txt. Consider removing those sections too for consistency.

resolution.recommendedReason: Directly addresses the reported issue with zero risk. The file is manually maintained and no regeneration step is needed.

actionability.reason: Valid bug — stale license entry needs removal. Trivial fix that should be included in the next release.

actions.description: Acknowledge the report and confirm the fix is straightforward
actions.comment:
Thanks for catching this. You're correct — PR #3463 removed libmicrohttpd from Skia's DEPS but missed updating `External-Dependency-Info.txt`. Since this file is packed as `THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.txt` in every NuGet package, the stale entry is currently shipping to all consumers.

The fix is straightforward: remove the libmicrohttpd section (lines 1475-1988) from `External-Dependency-Info.txt`. There are also a few stale entries in `scripts/guardian/CredScanSuppressions.json` that reference the now-removed microhttpd paths.

We'll also review whether other dependencies removed in #3463 (imgui, SPIR-V Headers, SPIR-V Tools) need similar cleanup.
